userStories.title: Fix spawn RPC data race in driver.ts
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the spawn RPC response to use proper synchronization so the child exit code doesn't race with subsequent RPC calls.
acceptanceCriteria: Fix managed.wait().then() in driver.ts _handleSyscall spawn case to not write to dataBuf without Atomics notification
Child exit code must be delivered through a mechanism that doesn't race with the worker's next RPC call
Option A: use a separate SharedArrayBuffer slot for async spawn results
Option B: make spawn synchronous by blocking until child exits (simpler but limits parallelism)
Test: rapid spawn/wait cycles don't produce incorrect exit codes
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: driver.ts lines ~664-667: after kernel.spawn(), managed.wait().then((code) => { view.setInt32(0, code, true); }) writes to the shared data buffer asynchronously. By the time the child exits, the worker may have issued another RPC call using the same dataBuf, causing data corruption. The write has no Atomics.notify() to wake the worker.

Tests pass
userStories.notes: wasi-polyfill.ts lines ~1303-1306: clock subscriptions are completed immediately without any delay. nanosleep() from C goes through poll_oneoff and returns instantly. acceptanceCriteria: Make ProcessEntry.cwd mutable in packages/kernel/src/process-table.ts
Add chdir(pid, path) to KernelInterface in packages/kernel/src/types.ts
Implement chdir in kernel.ts: validate path exists and is a directory via VFS stat, update ProcessEntry.cwd
chdir to nonexistent path throws ENOENT
chdir to file (not directory) throws ENOTDIR
Guard with ownership check — only the process's own driver can chdir
Child process spawned after chdir inherits the updated cwd
Test: chdir then getcwd returns new path
Test: chdir then spawn child — child cwd matches
Test: chdir to bad path returns ENOENT
Test: chdir to file returns ENOTDIR
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Currently cwd is immutable after process creation. Shell cd works inside brush-shell but the kernel's getcwd(pid) returns the original cwd. Files: packages/kernel/src/process-table.ts (ProcessEntry), packages/kernel/src/kernel.ts (implement chdir), packages/kernel/src/types.ts (KernelInterface).

userStories.notes: Currently env is immutable after process creation. Env is copied to child as { ...parentEnv, ...overrides }. Files: packages/kernel/src/process-table.ts, packages/kernel/src/kernel.ts, packages/kernel/src/types.ts.

userStories.title: SIGPIPE on broken pipe write
userStories.description: As a developer, I need writing to a closed pipe to deliver SIGPIPE to the writer before raising EPIPE, so pipelines like 'yes | head -1' terminate correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: PipeManager.write() calls processTable.kill(pid, SIGPIPE) before throwing EPIPE when read end is closed
PipeManager needs access to ProcessTable — inject via constructor or add pid parameter to write()
Default SIGPIPE action: terminate the process with exit code 128+13=141
Test: create pipe, close read end, write to write end — writer receives SIGPIPE and exits 141
Test: pipeline where reader exits early — writer terminates via SIGPIPE (not hangs)
Test: verify EPIPE error is still thrown after SIGPIPE delivery
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Critical POSIX gap. pipe-manager.ts line 87-88 throws EPIPE but never sends SIGPIPE. Without this, pipelines with early-exit readers (head, grep -m1) don't terminate the writer. PipeManager currently has no reference to ProcessTable — needs constructor injection or a callback.

userStories.title: POSIX wstatus encoding for waitpid
userStories.description: As a developer, I need waitpid to return POSIX-encoded status so WIFEXITED/WEXITSTATUS/WIFSIGNALED/WTERMSIG macros work correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: Add exitReason: 'normal' | 'signal' and exitSignal?: number to ProcessEntry
When process exits normally: exitReason='normal', exitCode=code
When process killed by signal: exitReason='signal', exitSignal=signalNumber
waitpid returns POSIX wstatus: normal exit = (exitCode << 8), signal death = (signalNumber & 0x7F)
Create packages/kernel/src/wstatus.ts with WIFEXITED, WEXITSTATUS, WIFSIGNALED, WTERMSIG helpers
Export helpers from kernel package
Test: normal exit(42) — WIFEXITED=true, WEXITSTATUS=42
Test: killed by SIGKILL — WIFSIGNALED=true, WTERMSIG=9
Test: killed by SIGTERM — WIFSIGNALED=true, WTERMSIG=15
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Currently waitpid returns raw exit code number. POSIX encodes how the process terminated (normal vs signal) into the status word. The C parity tests (kill_child.c) use WIFEXITED/WIFSIGNALED macros which depend on this encoding. Files: packages/kernel/src/process-table.ts, new file packages/kernel/src/wstatus.ts.

Tests pass
userStories.notes: Files: packages/kernel/src/fd-table.ts (FileDescription, fork method), packages/kernel/src/kernel.ts (fdOpen, new methods), packages/kernel/src/types.ts. Currently all FDs are inherited. The kernel has a heuristic close for stdio pipe dups but no general mechanism.

userStories.title: SIGTSTP/SIGCONT - Ctrl+Z job control
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Ctrl+Z to suspend the foreground process and SIGCONT to resume it, enabling shell job control (fg/bg/jobs).
acceptanceCriteria: Add 'stopped' to ProcessEntry status: 'running' | 'stopped' | 'exited'

SIGSTOP (19): same as SIGTSTP but cannot be caught (same behavior in our model)

Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: For WasmVM: SIGTSTP is effectively tracked in kernel state (status='stopped') but the WASM Worker can't truly pause (synchronous execution). Shell job tracking (fg/bg/jobs builtins) depends on kernel state, not actual Worker suspension. Files: packages/kernel/src/process-table.ts, packages/kernel/src/pty.ts.

userStories.title: TCP socket implementation - connect, send, recv, close

Tests pass
userStories.notes: The main thread (driver.ts) has access to Node.js net module for real TCP connections. The WASM worker communicates via SharedArrayBuffer RPC (same pattern as VFS read/write). Key challenge: recv must block the worker via Atomics.wait until data arrives. Use the same signalBuf/dataBuf pattern as existing RPC. Create a simple TCP echo server in the test for verification.

userStories.notes: Layer TLS on top of existing TCP socket from US-070. The socket table entry tracks whether a socket is plain TCP or TLS. Use a test HTTPS server (Node https.createServer with self-signed cert) for testing.

userStories.title: wasi-libc patches for socket functions

userStories.title: select/poll emulation via host_net blocking RPC

userStories.notes: Full select/poll/epoll is not possible in JS, but we can emulate basic poll semantics. The main thread checks socket state (readable/writable/error) and returns results.
